Page 1 of 7 
CSR POLICY 2025 
1.0 INTRODUCTION 
1.1 ONGC conducts its business as a responsible corporate and believes in holistically addressing all 
issues related to People, Planet and Profit for a sustainable business and better future for all living 
beings. ONGC is committed to achieve inclusive growth of the marginalized and deprived sections of 
the society through its CSR initiatives to be implemented within the geographical boundaries of 
India, with preference to its Operational Areas, by supplementing government’s effort and / or by 
making independent efforts. 
1.2 Vision 
To support responsible and sustainable initiatives, while taking care of the concern for People, Planet 
and Profit. 
1.3 Mission 
- Continually enhance the triple bottom line benchmarks of economic, environmental and social 
performance through committed involvement of the employees and other relevant stakeholders. 
- To develop social wealth for the communities we engage with. 
- Promote efficient usage of scarce resources, encourage green energy initiatives and develop 
innovative solutions to fulfil the vision by stepping beyond the mandatory provisions.  
2.0 ABBREVIATIONS / DEFINITIONS 
2.1 "Act" means the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013) and all its subsequent amendments. Any 
subsequent revisions to the Companies (Amendment) Act, 2020 will be construed to be accepted by 
ONGC. References to particular sections of the Act shall be automatically construed to refer to 
relevant sections in the amended Act. 
2.2 “MCA/DPE Guidelines ” means any guidelines/FAQs/notifications issued by the Ministry of 
Corporate Affairs, Government of India or Department of Public Enterprises, Ministry of Finance, 
Government of India 
2.3 “Rules” means the Companies (CSR Policy) Rules, 2014 issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs 
(MCA), as amended from time to time. Any subsequent revisions to the Companies (Corporate Social 
Responsibility Policy) Amendment Rules, 2025 will be construed to be accepted by ONGC. 
2.4 The following terms used in this policy statement are applied as per their definitions given in the Act 
or Rules  
i. Administrative overheads 
ii. Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) 
iii. CSR Committee 
iv. CSR Policy 
v. Net profit 
vi. Ongoing Project 
vii. Public Authority 
viii. International Organization 
3.0 PURPOSE 
- This CSR policy is a policy statement containing the intent, approach and direction given by the 
board of ONGC, taking into account the recommendations of its CSR Committee, and includes 
guiding principles for selection, implementation and monitoring of ac tivities as well as 
Page 2 of 7 
formulation of the annual action plan, mode of implementation of CSR projects, monitoring 
mechanisms to monitor implementation of CSR projects of ONGC and assess the impact of CSR 
projects on Sustainable Development Goals; 
- To envision, execute and manage long enduring projects of up to 3 years duration (excluding the 
year of commencement) , or the limits prescribed by the Rules in vogue. The projects shall be 
undertaken with a wide geographical spread and larger social impact. The focus shall be on 
inclusive growth of marginalized and deprived sections of the society as well as achievement of 
Sustainable Development Goals. 
4.0 SCOPE & INTERPRETATION 
4.1 ONGC has developed this Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Policy in consonance with the CSR 
Policy framework enshrined in section 135 of the Act, and in accordance with the Rules and 
Guidelines on CSR for Central Public Sector Enterprises issued from time to time by MCA/DPE. 
4.2 It shall apply to all CSR Projects undertaken by ONGC as per liberal interpretation of activities listed 
in Schedule-VII of the Act, within the geographical limits of India alone, except for training of Indian 
sports personnel representing any State or Union territory at national level or India at international 
level and preferably towards the benefit of marginalized, disadvantaged, poor and deprived sections 
of the community and the environment and achievement of Sustainable Development Goals.  
4.3 Any point not covered by this Policy would be interpreted in accordance with the existing Rules and 
latest MCA/DPE Guidelines, with regard of CSR implementation by CPSEs, with the Rules and 
MCA/DPE Guidelines taking precedence over the ONGC CSR Policy in case of any conflict. 
5.0 GOVERNANCE 
5.1 Board of ONGC , taking into account the recommendations of its CSR Committee, will devise guiding 
principles for selection, implementation and monitoring of activities as well as formulation of the 
annual action plan of CSR activities in ONGC, mode of impl ementation of CSR projects, monitoring 
mechanisms to monitor implementation of CSR projects of ONGC and assess the impact of its CSR 
projects on Sustainable Development Goals; 
5.2 The powers for approval and sanction of individual projects shall be as per the delegated powers in 
the Book of Delegated Powers, as approved by the Board of ONGC and as modified from time to time. 
5.3 In case of ongoing project, Board of ONGC, through its delegated powers, shall  
(a) monitor the implementation of the project with reference to the approved timelines and year-
wise allocation and  
(b) be competent to make modifications, if any, for smooth implementation of the project within 
the overall permissible time period. 
5.4 Board of ONGC shall ensure that Administrative Overheads shall not exceed 5% of the total CSR 
Expenditure of the company for the financial year, or as per the limits prescribed in the Rules in 
vogue; 
5.5 The CSR Committee of the Board shall formulate and recommend to the Board, an annual action plan 
in pursuance of its CSR policy, which shall include the following, namely: - 
(a) the list of CSR projects that are approved to be undertaken in areas or subjects specified in 
the Schedule VII of the Act; 
(b) the manner of execution of such projects as specified in sub -rule (1) of rule 4 of the Rules, 
will be followed by ONGC in this regard; 
(c) the modalities of utilisation of funds and implementation schedules for the projects;  
(d) monitoring and reporting mechanism for the projects; and 
Page 3 of 7 
(e) details of need and impact assessment, if any, for the projects undertaken by the ONGC: 
Provided that Board of ONGC may alter such plan at any time during the financial year, as per 
the recommendation of its CSR Committee of the board, based on the reasonable justification 
to that effect. 
5.6 Board of ONGC may alter Annual Action Plan of CSR activities at any time during the financial year, 
as per the recommendation of its CSR Committee of the board, based on the reasonable justification 
to that effect. 
5.7 The Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at work centres will be responsible 
for administering and executing the policy. As ONGC CSR activities evolve further and in line with 
MCA/DPE Guidelines and Amendments issued from time to time, the policy may be revised with the 
approval of the Board of ONGC based on recommendations of the CSR Committee.  
6.0 CSR ACTIVITIES 
6.1 CSR Projects will be identified by the Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at 
work centres across India, as per the criteria set in para 4.2 of this policy.  
6.2 Budgets will be allocated for CSR projects through a process incorporating identification of suitable 
implementation agencies, need assessment (wherever required) and clear outlining of desired 
outcomes of CSR projects.  
6.3 All CSR activities will be in in projectised mode, which will, as far as feasible, entail the following 
components: 
i. Need Based Assessment / Baseline survey / Study where considered necessary / feasible;  
ii. Identification of specific and measurable objectives / goals in identified sectors and 
geographies; 
iii. Formation of the project and preparation of Detailed Project Report (DPR);  
iv. Identification of time lines – clear specification of start date and end date;  
v. Specification of annual financial allocation; 
vi. Clear identification of beneficiaries (by name where possible); 
vii. Clear identification of milestones for the complete duration of the Project;  
viii. Preparation and signing of agreement with Implementing Agencies;  
ix. Preparation and implementation of a comprehensive and concurrent documentation 
procedure; 
x. Robust, periodic review & monitoring; 
xi. Evaluation & Assessment, preferably both concurrent and final (wherever possible, by a 
competent third party); 
xii. Mandatory Reporting on Management Information System devised for the purpose of 
monitoring progress of CSR projects. 
6.4 Opportunities for implementing projects which complement / supplement Government initiatives / 
programmes will be explored. However, no direct contribution will be made to such initiatives, 
except where the Act / Rules specify such allocation. 
6.5 ONGC will lay emphasis on the sustainability of its projects to ensure they remain relevant and viable 
even upon disengagement at the end of the project period. 
6.6 ONGC will explore possibilities for collaborating / co -operating with other Corporate / National / 
Multi- lateral / Bi -lateral Agencies to synergise its efforts and increase both financial resources as 
Page 4 of 7 
well as outcomes and impact in such a manner that the CSR Committee is in a position to report 
separately on such projects in accordance with the rules. 
7.0 CSR PLANNING 
7.1 To assist in planning of the activities, the indicative budget allocation for broad sector of Activities 
will be delineated in the Annual Action Plan which shall be formulated and recommended by the CSR 
Committee and submitted to the Board for approval as per para 5.5 of this policy  
7.2 The Guidelines issued by the MCA/DPE from time to time for spending CSR in specific focus areas 
shall also be considered for forming the Annual Action Plan for of CSR projects.  
7.3 ONGC shall give preference to well defined Project operating principles during the planning stage 
for the identification and implementation of its CSR Projects to ensure optimal utilisation of the CSR 
budget. 
7.4 ONGC shall endeavour to understand the stakeholder expectations through a structured engagement 
process and communication strategy and shall leverage this understanding for betterment of all the 
stakeholders. 
8.0 CSR IMPLEMENTATION 
8.1 Implementation of the CSR projects shall be  by ONGC itself,  through the ONGC Foundation , or by 
implementing agencies fulfilling the criteria laid down under the Rules. Implementing agencies shall 
necessarily be fulfilling the criteria specified in Annexure -1 of this Policy. Only agencies with a valid 
CSR-1 registration number will be engaged for CSR project implementation.  
8.2 The CSR process of ONGC has been digitalised with the launch of the ONGC CSR portal 
www.ongccsr.co.in to make the process transparent and efficient. With effect from. 01-Apr-2024, all 
implementing agencies shall be required to register themselves on the portal and proposals shall be 
accepted through the portal only. 
8.3 ONGC will endeavour at all times to build and develop the skills of its CSR team and enhance level of 
CSR awareness within the organization and may also engage International Organizations for 
capacity building of its own CSR personnel. 
9.0 CSR BUDGET AND EXPENDITURE 
9.1 The Board of ONGC will ensure that in each Financial Year (FY), at least two percent of the average 
net profit (calculated as per Section 198 of the Act ) made during the three immediately preceding 
Financial Years is spent on CSR activities / projects. 
9.2 The Board of ONGC will satisfy itself that the funds disbursed have been utilised for the purposes 
and in the manner as approved by it and the Director (Finance) shall certify to the effect.  
9.3 In the event, that amount indicated in para 9.1 above is not spent in its entirety in that Financial Year, 
the reasons thereof will be outlined as per section 134 (3) (o) of the Act to be shared with all the 
stakeholders through the Annual Report and the u nspent amount shall be transferred as per the 
provisions of section 135 (6) of the Act. 
9.4 Any surplus or profit arising out of CSR activities / projects shall be dealt with as per Rule 7(2) of 
the CSR Rules, or the Rules in vogue. Rule 7(2) presently states that such surplus shall not form part 
of the business profit of the company and shall be ploughed back into the same project or shall be 
transferred to the unspent CSR Account and spent in pursuance of CSR Policy and Annual Action Plan 
of the Company or transferred to a fun d specified in Schedule VII, within a period of six months of 
the expiry of the financial year. 
9.5 In an event where CSR expenditure is in excess of requirement under section 135 (5) of the Act, such 
excess amount may be set-off against the requirement to spend under section 135 (5) of the Act, up 
to immediate succeeding three financial years subject to the condition that  - 
Page 5 of 7 
(a) this excess amount shall not include any surplus arising out of CSR projects; and  
(b) Board of ONGC shall pass a resolution to this effect 
9.6 Any capital asset created/acquired out of CSR funds shall be held by such entities as defined in the 
Rules in vogue, namely 
(a) a company established under section 8 of the Act, or a registered Public Trust or Registered 
Society having charitable objects and CSR Registration Number, or  
(b) beneficiaries of the said project, in the form of self- help groups, collectives, entities or  
(c) a Public Authority; 
9.7 Expenditure towards Impact Assessment will be booked towards Corporate Social Responsibility for 
that financial year, which shall not exceed two percent of the total CSR expenditure for that financial 
year (in which impact assessment is being carried out) or fifty lakh rupees, whichever is higher, or 
as per the limits prescribed by the Rules in vogue. 
9.8 In CSR Projects funded jointly by ONGC and  other companies / sources , CSR expenditure shall be 
claimed only for funds disbursed and utilised by ONGC. 
10.0 MONITORING & EVALUATION 
10.1 A comprehensive Monitoring mechanism will be devised by ONGC to ensure that the CSR process 
functions as mandated by the Act and the Rules, ensuring that all Projects are duly implemented as 
budgeted. This will be done on the basis of the following: 
• Engaging National & International Organizations, if required, for designing, monitoring and 
evaluation of the CSR projects  
• Allocation of a separate budget for setting up and running the monitoring system;  
• Installation of an electronic MIS and human architecture that shall work together to ensure a 
firm check on spends and the actual implementation of activities as planned;  
• Release of funds only against verified utilisations. 
10.2 The monitoring system devised by ONGC will include: 
• Field visits to Project sites by ONGC official(s) for projects with a financial outlay of Rs. 3 crore 
and above. For other cases, field visits may be conducted as per merit; 
• Comprehensive documentation / compilation of Field Visit Reports; 
• Regular interaction with beneficiary communities to obtain feedback;  
• Monitoring of timely fund utilization to ensure that Projects as budgeted are actually being 
carried out and/or 
• Any other activity that the CSR Committee may deem necessary in the larger interest of its 
CSR initiatives. 
• The monitoring of the projects shall be carried out and quarterly Action Taken Reports (ATRs) 
shall be submitted to the  Board. Professional agencies  may also be hired for carrying out 
Monitoring & Evaluation. 
11.0 IMPACT ASSESSMENT/EVALUATION OF CSR 
11.1 In order to assess the impact of its CSR Projects, maximize outcomes and build - in sustainability, 
scalability and replicability, ONGC shall undertake the following activities:  
• Wherever possible, Base line data shall be collected before start of the project.  
Page 6 of 7 
• As far as possible efforts will be made to use Quasi Experimental Research design for 
assessing the impact of CSR projects. 
• Impact Assessment, through an independent agency, of the CSR projects having outlay of Rs. 
1 Cr. or more and which have completed not less than one year before undertaking the impact 
study, or as mandated in the Rules in vogue. 
• Placing before the Board, the Impact Assessment reports and annexing the same to the annual 
report on CSR 
12.0 REPORTING 
12.1 ONGC will make a full report of its CSR projects undertaken during the previous year in the format 
prescribed for the “Annual Report on CSR Activities to be included in the Board’s Report”  as per  
Rules and other formats as required by MCA/DPE guidelines. 
13.0 DOCUMENTATION 
13.1 ONGC will endeavour at all times to put in place, for all projects, an effective documentation process. 
The comprehensive documentation would include – printed material, audio recordings, video 
recordings etc. 
13.2 Also, Project completion report documenting the key project parameters, implementation process, 
deliverables envisaged and the outcome achieved along with end line data and the suggestions / 
recommendations for scalability and replicability of the project may be developed wherever feasible. 
13.3 Documentation of all data generated during project execution shall be maintained by the CSR 
department. 
14.0 DISCLOSURE 
ONGC shall comply with Section 135 (2), 135 (4) (1) and 134 (3) (o) of the Act thereby ensuring that 
it makes a full disclosure of its CSR Policy, Projects, Activities, Expenditure details as well as the 
composition of the CSR Committee of the Board on the  corporate website of ONGC i.e. 
www.ongcindia.com. 
15.0 ACCOUNTING AND AUDITING 
15.1 ONGC will follow the Accounting and Auditing Guidance Note / Standards / Circulars / Notifications 
duly issued / approved by MCA/DPE and Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI), if any. 
15.2 Both Financial and non -Financial audit of CSR activities and projects shall be done in accordance 
with the Guidelines issued by MCA / DPE / ICAI. 
  
Page 7 of 7 
Annexure 1 
CRITERIA TO BE FULFILLED BY IMPLEMENTING AGENCIES  
1. Implementing agency must be an entity eligible for implementing CSR projects as defined in Rule 4 of 
the Companies (CSR) Rules, 2014 and its subsequent amendments, viz.  
a) ONGC Foundation, the trust set up by ONGC, especially for carrying out the CSR activities of ONGC 
and to manage the CSR assets created by ONGC, duly registered under the Indian Trust Act, 1882 
vide registration no. 1120 in Book No. 4 Vol. No. 1560 with PAN  no. AAAT04581A and CSR -1 
registration no. CSR00000594 as per the provisions contained under Rule 4(2) of the Rules;  
b) a company established under section 8 of the Act or a registered trust or a registered society, 
established by the Central Government or State Government;  
c) any entity established under an Act of Parliament or a State legislature;  
d) a company established under section 8 of the Act, or a registered public trust or a registered 
society; exempted under  sub-clauses (iv), (v), (vi) or (via) of clause (23C) of section 10 or 
registered under section 12A and approved under 80G of the Income Tax Act, 1961, and having 
an established track record of at least three years in undertaking similar activities  
Note: For the purpose of clause (c), the term “entity” shall mean a statutory body constituted under an Act 
of Parliament or State legislature to undertake activities covered in Schedule VII of the Act.   
2. CSR-1 registration and NGO Darpa n registration shall be mand atory for agencies registered under 
clauses (a) and (d) above 
3. Agencies blacklisted  on NGO Darpan portal of the Niti Aayog, Government of India, shall not be 
engaged as Implementing Agencies of CSR projects by ONGC 
4. All Implementing Partners will have to furnish the relevant information pertaining to the eligibility 
criteria as per the Rules, in addition to the information already required to be furnished on the ONGC 
CSR Portal. 
5. Agencies not fulfilling the criteria as mentioned above will not be considered as Implementing 
Agencies for CSR Projects. 
6. For implementing agencies fulfilling criteria under 1(d) above, maximum financial assistance in a CSR 
project shall be determined based on track record of the implementing agency. Track record shall be 
evaluated as per ONGC’s internal guidelines. 
7. In case of any ambiguity arising in the CSR Policy and the ONGC CSR Portal, CSR Policy shall supersede 
the ONGC CSR Portal.

9. Terms and Conditions: 
 
➢ Engagement on stipend basis does not guarantee any employment in ONGC 
➢ Sportspersons will commit to participate wherever required by ONGC. 
➢ Sportspersons must wear ONGC-logo on attire during tournaments where permitted. 
➢ Sportspersons must maintain discipline and ONGC’s code of conduct on & off the field. 
➢ Any disciplinary action or doping violation will lead to immediate termination of Stipend. 
 
10. Liability waiver: 
 
ONGC shall neither be liable for any loss / damage / injury / disability caused to any sportsperson on 
stipend  nor shall indemnify for any of such loss / damage / injury / disability which may arise or occur 
while performing any such sports activity.   
 
11. Termination of Stipend: 
 
The engagement on stipend can be terminated from either side by giving 1 months notice. 
However, ONGC reserves the right to terminate  the stipend of any sportsperson due to false 
information, fake certificate, misconduct, indiscipline etc. without any prior notice.  
 
12. Acceptance of Stipend: 
 
Offer letter for engagement  on stipend basis shall be sent via email for acceptance and following 
relevant documents will be required within 7 days from issue of offer letter:   
➢ ECS Form  
➢ PAN Card  
➢ Cancelled Cheque for Bank details   
➢ Letter of Acceptance of Offer.  
➢ Undertaking of Non-receipt of any other stipend / contract / scholarship from Central / State Govt., 
Public Sector Enterprises, Foundations / Private organisations.  
 
13. Withdrawal of Stipend: 
 
If a selected sportspersons does not submit the desired confirmation / information / documents within 15 
days, their offer of stipend shall be withdrawn automatically and will be extended to waiting list 
sportsperson of same sports.

ओएनजीसी में स्टाइपेंड आधार पर 2  हॉकी खिलाड़ियोों क े  डनयोजन (कोई स्थायी भर्ती नहीों) क े  डलए पात्रर्ता, चयन मानदोंड, 
डनयम एवों शर्तें। 
 
आवेदन क े वल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से ओएनजीसी क े  आधिकारिक ई-मेल ongcsports@ongc.co.in पि ही स्वीकाि धकए जाए ं गे। 
 
ओएनजीसी क े  पास डकसी अभ्यथी द्वारा फजी प्रमाण-पत्र प्रस्तुर्त डकए जाने पर ऐसे अभ्यथी का आवेदन डनरस्त करने र्तथा 
उसक े  डवरुद्ध उपयुक्त कानूनी काररवाई डकए जाने का अडधकार सुरडिर्त है। 
 
आवेदन अवडध : 04.03.2026 (1200 बजे) से 10.03.2026 (1800 बजे) तक अनुबोंध-1 में संलग्न प्रारूप में प्रस्तुत किें।

तक) 
 
1. उद्देश्य 
ओएनजीसी की खेल टीमों में कमी को पूिा किने तथा उन्हें सुदृढ़ किने क े  उद्देश्य से उत्क ृ ष्ट खखलाध़ियों को स्टाइपेंड आिाि पि 
धनयोधजत किना , ता धक िा ष्टर ीय एवं अखख ल भा ितीय स्ति की प्रधतयोधगता ओं में धनिंति भा गीदा िी एवं प्रधतस्पिा ात्मक प्रदशान सुधनधित 
धकया जा सक े ।

➢ धपछले दो वषों (1 जनवरी 2024 से आवेदन की अोंडर्तम डर्तडथ र्तक) में आयोधजत प्रधतयोधगताओं में खखला़िी क े  प्रदशान पि 
धवचाि धकया जाएगा।

िोजगाि/स्टाइपेंड/अनुबंि/छात्रवृधि पि नहीं होना चाधहए। 
 
4. आयु मानदोंड 
खखला़िी की आयु 18 से 30 वर्र क े  मध्य होनी चाधहए। न्यूनतम एवं अधिकतम आयु की गणना 1 फरवरी 2026 क े  आिाि पि की 
जाएगी।  आयु प्रमाण हेर्तु जन्म प्रमाण-पत्र / पासपोट्र / 10वीों किा का प्रमाण-पत्र (कोई एक) मान्य होगा। 
 
5. स्टाइपेंड पर डनयोजन की अवडध 
चयधनत खखलाध़ियों को प्रस्ताव पत्र जािी होने की धतधथ से 1 वषा की अवधि क े  धलए स्टाइपेंड आिाि पि धनयोधजत धकया जाएगा, धजसे

पर सहभाडगर्ता ओएनजीसी में डकसी भी प्रकार की डनयुखक्त की गारोंट्ी नहीों देर्ती।

• खेल ट र ायल : अडधकर्तम 30 अोंक 
• क ु ल : 100 अोंक 
 
डट्प्पणी: खेल ट र ायल में 22.5 अंक (उपलि अंकों का 75%) से कम प्राप्त किने वाले खखला़िी चयन हेतु पात्र नहीं होंगे। 
 
9. डनयम एवों शर्तें 
➢ स्टाइपेंड आिाि पि धनयोजन ओएनजीसी में रोजगार की गारोंट्ी नहीों है। 
➢ खखला़िी ओएनजीसी द्वािा आवश्यकतानुसाि सभी प्रधतयोधगताओं में भाग लेने क े  धलए प्रधतबद्ध होंगे। 
➢  जहााँ अनुमधत हो, प्रधतयोधगताओं क े  दौिान ओएनजीसी लोगो (प्रतीक-धचह्न युक्त परििान पहनना अधनवाया होगा। 
➢ खखलाध़ियों को मैदान क े  भीति एवं बाहि ओएनजीसी की आचाि संधहता एवं अनुशासन का पालन किना होगा।  
➢ धकसी भी प्रकाि की अनुशासनात्मक कािावाई या डोधपंग उल्लंर्न की खस्थधत में स्टाइपेंड तत्काल समाप्त कि धदया जाएगा। 
 
10. दाडयत्व अस्वीकरण 
 
ओएनजीसी धकसी भी स्टाइपेंडिािी खखला़िी को खेल गधतधवधियों क े  दौिान होने वाली धकसी भी प्रकाि की 
हाधन/क्षधत/चोट/धवकलांगता क े  धलए उििदायी नहीं होगा औि खेल गधतधवधि क े  दौिान ऐसी धकसी हाधन/क्षधत/चोट/धवकलांगता क े  
धलए क्षधतपूधता किेगा। 
 
11. स्टाइपेंड की समाखप्त 
स्टाइपेंड धनयोजन को धकसी भी पक्ष द्वािा 1 माह की सूचना देकि समाप्त धकया जा सकता है।  
हालााँधक, फजी जानकािी , फजी प्रमाण-पत्र, कदाचाि , अनुशासनहीनता आधद क े  कािण ओएनजीसी  धबना पूवा सूचना क े  स्टाइपेंड 
समाप्त किने का अधिकाि सुिधक्षत िखता है। 
 
12. स्टाइपेंड की स्वीक ृ डर्त 
स्टाइपेंड आिाि पि धनयोजन क े  धलए प्रस्ताव पत्र ई -मेल क े  माध्यम से भेजा जाएगा।  
प्रस्ताव पत्र जािी होने की धतधथ से 7 धदनों क े  भीति धनम्नधलखखत दस्तावेज प्रस्तुत किना अधनवाया होगा: 
➢ ईसीएस फॉमा 
➢ पैन काडा  
➢ बैंक धवविण हेतु धनिस्त चेक 
➢ प्रस्ताव स्वीक ृ धत पत्र 
➢ यह शपथ -पत्र धक अभ्यथी धकसी अन्य क ें द्रीय/िाज्य सिकाि, सावाजधनक क्षेत्र उपक्रम, फाउंडेशन या धनजी संगठन से कोई 
स्टाइपेंड/अनुबंि/छात्रवृधि प्राप्त नहीं कि िहा/िही है। 
 
13.  स्टाइपेंड की वापसी 
यधद चयधनत खखला़िी 15 धदनों क े  भीति आवश्यक पुधष्ट/जानकािी/दस्तावेज प्रस्तुत नहीं किता/किती है, तो उसका स्टाइपेंड 
प्रस्ताव स्वतः  धनिस्त कि धदया जाएगा औि उसी खेल की प्रतीक्षा सूची में शाधमल खखला़िी को प्रदान धकया जाएगा। 
 
 
कारपोरेट् िेल प्रभाग, ओएनजीसी 
अनुबोंध – 1 
 
ऑयल ए ों ड नेचुरल गैस कॉरपोरेशन डलडमट्ेड 
स्टाइपेंड आधार पर खिलाड़ियोों क े  डनयोजन हेर्तु आवेदन प्रपत्र 
(यह स्टाइपेंड ओएनजीसी में रोजगार की गारोंट्ी नहीों देर्ता) 
(सभी डववरण स्पष्ट् अिरोों में भरें) 
 
क.  िेल:  पासपोटा आकाि का िंगीन 
फोटो धचपकाए ाँ   
(6 माह से अधिक पुिाना न हो)  
ि.  िेलने का स्थान:  
ग.  आवेदक का नाम: स्क ू ल प्रमाणपत्र क े

कोई जानकािी असत्य/गलत पाई जाती है या मेिी अपात्रता पाई जाती है , तो मेिा चयन धनिस्त/समाप्त धकया जा सकता है तथा मेिे धवरुद्ध उपयुक्त

analysis.overview: The ONGC Startup Fund is a corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiative by Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) aimed at supporting startups through financial assistance and mentorship. It operates under ONGC's CSR policy to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, particularly in sectors aligned with energy and sustainability. The fund provides grants to eligible startups for project development and scaling, with a focus on inclusive growth and sustainable development goals.
objectives: Support responsible and sustainable initiatives while addressing People, Planet and Profit
Enhance triple bottom line benchmarks of economic, environmental and social performance
Develop social wealth for communities engaged with ONGC
Promote efficient usage of scarce resources and encourage green energy initiatives
Develop innovative solutions to fulfill vision by stepping beyond mandatory CSR provisions
analysis.eligibility: CSR Projects will be identified by the Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at work centres across India, as per the criteria set in para 4.2 of this policy. It shall apply to all CSR Projects undertaken by ONGC as per liberal interpretation of activities listed in Schedule-VII of the Act, within the geographical limits of India alone, except for training of Indian sports personnel representing any State or Union territory at national level or India at international level and preferably towards the benefit of marginalized, disadvantaged, poor and deprived sections of the community and the environment and achievement of Sustainable Development Goals.
analysis.benefits: Financial support for CSR projects, capacity building of CSR personnel through engagement with International Organizations, transparent and efficient process via the ONGC CSR portal (www.ongccsr.co.in), monitoring and evaluation of projects, impact assessment for projects with outlay of Rs. 1 Cr. or more, and reporting through annual CSR report included in Board’s Report.
analysis.financial_support: The Board of ONGC will ensure that in each Financial Year (FY), at least two percent of the average net profit (calculated as per Section 198 of the Act) made during the three immediately preceding Financial Years is spent on CSR activities/projects. Funds are disbursed to implementing agencies fulfilling criteria laid down under the Rules, requiring valid CSR-1 registration number. Expenditure towards Impact Assessment will be booked towards CSR for that financial year, not exceeding two percent of total CSR expenditure or fifty lakh rupees, whichever is higher.
analysis.application_process: 1. Identify CSR project aligned with Schedule VII of Companies Act, 2013 2. Prepare Detailed Project Report (DPR) with need-based assessment, objectives, beneficiary details, timelines, budget break-up 3. Submit proposal through ONGC CSR portal (www.ongccsr.co.in) with required documents 4. ONGC CSR Committee reviews and recommends to Board for approval 5. Upon approval, sign agreement with Implementing Agency 6. Disburse funds as per implementation schedule 7. Monitor project progress through field visits, documentation, beneficiary feedback 8. Conduct impact assessment for projects ≥ Rs. 1 Cr. 9. Submit utilization report and fund utilization certificate 10. Report outcomes in annual CSR report
required_documents: Organization Details (brief description, goals & objectives)
Details of similar projects ongoing/completed in previous three years
Impact assessment of previous projects in similar focus areas (if any)
Details of previous support received from ONGC
Details of largest project undertaken by agency in recent past with financial implication
Details of proposed CSR project
Brief about goals and objectives of the project
Report of Need Assessment/Baseline Survey (if any)
Brief about targeted beneficiaries
Methodology of beneficiary selection and number
Details about physical and social milestones/deliverables
Detail timeline/duration of the project
Budget estimate with detailed break-up and documents proving rate reasonability
Monitoring mechanism for project activities and benefits
Documents depicting funding sources other than ONGC and amounts funded by each
How sustainability of the project is planned
Under which sector of Schedule VII of Companies Act 2013 the project is covered
Documents proving ownership of land or lease agreement (if civil construction involved)
Copy of approved construction map and estimate vetted by certified Architect with NOC
Detail budget estimates of construction plan with drawings and bill of quantities
Documentary evidence of Land Use Conversion (if agriculture land involved)
Three quotations (inclusive of GST) for each capital item with installation cost
Affidavit (as per Annexure 1)
ECS Form (Format enclosed at Annexure 2)
Copy of Cancelled Cheque
Copy of PAN Card
Copy of Audited Account Statement of previous 3 financial years
Copy of Registration on NITI Aayog Darpan Portal (if any)
GST Number and GST Registration Certificate (if applicable)
Affidavit on Rs. 100 non-judiciary stamp paper duly notarized
Electronic Clearing Service (Credit Clearing) Form
analysis.deadlines: Rolling basis — proposals can be submitted throughout the year as per the annual action plan formulated and recommended by the CSR Committee and submitted to the Board for approval.
analysis.implementing_agency: Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at work centres across India
caveats: Administrative overheads shall not exceed 5% of total CSR expenditure
No direct contribution to Government initiatives unless specified by Act/Rules
Projects must be undertaken within geographical limits of India alone
Preference given to marginalized, disadvantaged, poor and deprived sections
Surplus from CSR projects shall not form part of business profit and must be ploughed back or transferred per Schedule VII
Impact assessment expenditure capped at 2% of total CSR expenditure or ₹50 lakh, whichever is higher
In jointly funded projects, CSR expenditure claimed only for funds disbursed and utilized by ONGC
